sn35.136:1.1: “Monks, devas & human beings take pleasure in forms, delight in forms, rejoice in forms.
sn35.136:1.2: With the change, fading away, & cessation of forms, devas & human beings dwell in suffering & stress.
sn35.136:1.4: “Devas & human beings take pleasure in sounds…
sn35.136:1.8: “Devas & human beings take pleasure in ideas, delight in ideas, rejoice in ideas.
sn35.136:1.9: With the change, fading away, & cessation of ideas, devas & human beings dwell in suffering & stress.
sn35.136:1.10: “But the Tathāgata, monks—worthy & rightly self-awakened—knowing, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and escape from—forms, doesn’t take pleasure in forms, delight in forms, rejoice in forms.
sn35.136:1.11: With the change, fading away, & cessation of forms, the Tathāgata dwells happily.
sn35.136:1.12: “Knowing, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and escape from—sounds…
sn35.136:1.16: “Knowing, as they have come to be, the origination, the disappearance, the allure, the drawbacks of—and escape from—ideas, he doesn’t take pleasure in ideas, delight in ideas, rejoice in ideas.
sn35.136:1.17: With the change, fading away, & cessation of ideas, the Tathāgata dwells happily.”
sn35.136:2.4: as long as they’re said to exist,
sn35.136:3.1: are supposed by the world together with its devas
sn35.136:3.4: that’s supposed by them to be stress.
sn35.136:4.2: is viewed by the noble ones as bliss.
sn35.136:4.4: runs counter to the whole world.
sn35.136:5.2: the noble ones say is stress.
sn35.136:6.1: See the Dhamma, hard to understand!
sn35.136:6.2: Here those who don’t know are confused.
sn35.136:6.3: For those who are veiled, it’s darkness,
sn35.136:6.4: blindness for those who don’t see.
sn35.136:7.1: But for the good it’s blatant,
sn35.136:7.2: like light for those who see.
sn35.136:7.4: they don’t understand it— dumb animals, unadept in the Dhamma.
sn35.136:8.1: It’s not easy for those overcome by passion for becoming,
sn35.136:8.2: flowing along in the stream of becoming,
sn35.136:8.4: to wake up to this Dhamma.
sn35.136:9.3: to this state?—the state that, through rightly knowing it,
